name: SAF-T/e-invoicing synthetic hero evaluation
scope_note: Synthetic portfolio evaluation. It compares a manual baseline estimate, a simple LLM baseline and the assisted workflow on one realistic synthetic case. It is not production evidence or legal accuracy evidence.

rows.interpretation: The assisted workflow is faster because it reuses extracted obligations and traceability templates.
Reviewer mode and claim risk checks reduce unsupported or overbroad statements.
Explicit obligation-to-QA mapping surfaces test gaps.
A higher correction rate is expected in the assisted workflow because the product exposes review decisions instead of hiding uncertainty.

rows.metric: Missing obligation recall on gold set
rows.interpretation: RAG plus reviewer corrections recover more expected obligations in the synthetic gold set.
Sentence-level claim risk review rewrites more risky customer-facing claims.

rows.unit: percent obligations with at least one mapped negative test
rows.interpretation: v19 blocks release-ready status unless every mapped obligation has a negative/edge/failure test.

headline.unsupported_claim_rate: 22% simple LLM → 6% assisted after reviewer pass
headline.negative_test_coverage: 0% happy-path manual baseline → 100% assisted mapped negative coverage

honesty_note: Synthetic one-case evaluation. Use to demonstrate measurement thinking, not to claim production accuracy.
